crime.quote: The waiter! At the restaurant! He slipped something in our drinks! That's why we passed out!

victim.value: Anny and Matt, young couple on a date
victim.quote: Mine kinda tastes funny...Mine too! It's really tangy!

closed_world.quote: We got up from our table and left the once crowded resaurant. The parking lot was deserted

culprit_and_motive.quote: Would you like refills on your drinks? It's on the house.

investigator.quote: I grabbed my phone from my purse and turned it on. Eleven messages!

red_herring.value: Initial assumption that drinks tasted off due to restaurant error rather than deliberate drugging
red_herring.quote: Hmmm...Mine kinda tastes funny,' I held up my Pepsi and examined it. It looked fine but it tasted sort of off.

clue_fairness.value: Reader experiences the suspicious drink taste simultaneously with the characters
clue_fairness.quote: Mine too! It's really tangy!' Matt took another sip, made a face and put it down.

social_world.value: Equestrian competition hierarchy with trainers, riders, and service staff power dynamics
social_world.quote: My trainer, Sarah, stood next to me as we waited for my turn.

alibi.value: The waiter's legitimate service role provides cover for poisoning drinks
alibi.quote: Would you like refills on your drinks? It's on the house.

reveal_mechanic.value: Retrospective realization connecting drink tampering to waiter's opportunity
reveal_mechanic.quote: The waiter! At the restaurant! He slipped something in our drinks!

investigator_wound.value: Protagonist's vulnerability through romantic attachment and trust in social situations
investigator_wound.quote: He smiled and reached over the table and grabbed my hand. It tingled with our touch

_meta.title: ebook mary mccausland anny 039 s clover
